name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Hunkered down, but not slowing down
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
Governor Holcomb updated Hoosiers again today, and he said that he’s inspired by the stories he’s hearing of Hoosiers across our state Friend,

As Hoosiers, we may be hunkered down, **but that doesn’t mean that we’re slowing down!**
Governor Holcomb updated Hoosiers again today, and he said that he’s inspired by the stories he’s hearing of Hoosiers across our state who are social distancing – but not social isolating: _“I hope you continue to be as inspired on a daily basis to read the stories from border to border, from Lake Michigan to the Ohio River, it's simply amazing to see the**heart of Hoosiers who are hunkered down right now, not slowing down to meet the needs of their neighbors.** And for as much as government is doing, it's individuals who are standing in the gap.”_
-Gov. Holcomb You’ve probably seen many of these stories. More than 1,000 businesses have reached out to your state government offering ways to help! We’ll send a bunch of inspiring tales of the Hoosier spirit – Hoosiers helping Hoosiers – to your inbox tomorrow. **[(For a preview, visit our Twitter page.)](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=095b1185d93d447783874ebbc33afbb9&_e=sWnkBpiX41BB_1JHCECkmsPFjsVJJsscBYGRWqQ6VO1f-bVd3LP98mLo9A0zFQtKwHmQhLsga11qvzzSnrj--YRImnsRi91B2FBUpbGUnzbcZnUyc0W3ZxOFGIjiDUVJNCxBNk6iBU2PNRe1It0j6Y1aWW2RYtueSujZp0SvDOZUZK3GSukeordKAIDP0YAjT3Sga3x5e-h7fYtLDLyBq72X_PsQ-x-qyZNKikbjOFfCqfG10G3sIsWCVf6M1a97Xm2B81dOsqQtJ7lf5DfshSKF1TrgMx0lN9g2Kx1m4u_QZtWAa_iQNvFdrePVY5BAUs2nY74Ha_5K9e0zWYE9jY2s9-f7OEDnUpcAAkHsHcVr3UHze0JoopsJtKqVzDRVPSO94R0EaXCfuYJSdpJs6g%3D%3D)**
But now, we wanted to give you a quick update on a few more actions Governor Holcomb and his team took today to help our fellow Hoosiers impacted by the coronavirus.
* **Temporarily unemployed?** Governor Holcomb wants to make sure you receive relief as quickly as possible. The Department of Workforce Development has suspended the required one-week waiting period before paying unemployment benefits, retroactive to March 8. You can apply for unemployment insurance online at **[www.unemployment.in.gov](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=095b1185d93d447783874ebbc33afbb9&_e=sWnkBpiX41BB_1JHCECkmgGvMcolYfsHpd3qTn3TRpqZS7xcm8APyMEp8t2zamC_s0q3Ty_c06CRT8MhmBXAislLsT1PmwKgP2HVLWGe5qiFQ58Ctp4lSrFGHbhMELI1Xj_n5LsWgh5aMma6ojGpPSvYEAp_OtJpfEc2pA6MxeXpYSNpcpxktKq4_UWL307FJph87u1TPT9DVVB8I8ub0_itMKZNA-k5p1BIkhctQmzVUUa4X-36fYAsQh8QB_TByaX8Ner1hF1qy-8s8WUZs99cW4ySY3RENkO5SSbgHTWsn1OxJV_GYUbBJMpVRMJvGp3pBTybmsNoI1LXSastazAnwgjBMbzzmlt8GPR_lT08w0MzuoduHvEecdYMwHe-Andw4WCccbaqbmb_IDXTng%3D%3D)[.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=095b1185d93d447783874ebbc33afbb9&_e=sWnkBpiX41BB_1JHCECkmgGvMcolYfsHpd3qTn3TRpqZS7xcm8APyMEp8t2zamC_s0q3Ty_c06CRT8MhmBXAislLsT1PmwKgP2HVLWGe5qiFQ58Ctp4lSrFGHbhMELI1Xj_n5LsWgh5aMma6ojGpPSvYEAp_OtJpfEc2pA6MxeXpYSNpcpxktKq4_UWL307FiMnQ2-Qm_o3CjcsZJ-TqsCwUfDUVi7cCQ-LEOd-X4TN-yodey99SayD0mnKQlh59a9vyCOu6yzZbOBLflvBHbqcNR3oyghxRQNbsw9W-dBRL8NJWkSRvbdHkdI40gCSF3wj0Trht1RqdRV8D7WQLF_lElrXmyxQ_1LoqBi-O_R_7wvtPqKysOJgBAHYExMote_k3FWTcIEyRVbyxY1DrWQ%3D%3D)**
* **Chronic health condition?** You’ll be able to receive a 90-day supply of your non-controlled prescription medication.
* **On Medicaid?** You can use your benefits to cover costs of using alternative forms of transportation, such as ride-sharing, for appointments to see healthcare providers.
* **Need a meal delivered?** The Family and Social Services Administration has additional funding flexibility to allow for this.
* **Local government?** The Department of Local Government Finance has extended some of your deadlines.

You can read today’s **[full executive order here.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=095b1185d93d447783874ebbc33afbb9&_e=sWnkBpiX41BB_1JHCECkmnei4JGWXmFKVEr3PAu-_kkGqRAXLMWKe2aFDdFZbvjAAUtPnhd56E6_M_4F_H9zM0q04lrxRXa_7f8_5DL9Exdy71xp8lU-Zp9gmDBBRVR4vzozXJVB1VuSKKaQCIlUcie-SwH4K-uVaJGVzm9cjzlWW_VtaLyc0GbmLuB8J9p4D5eN-lygrECV3asRtJww-sJQhQsYdLJLWKvtzUK8a6bWvaTCpeuNihg_On0PF2vnOpZopUCgHRJXEsgQxld1kN8GJB1AF6NnZnwEGq-sueCMQvuDKP07v42IwJPfq9bwjvfg0q4yuNtWkLsubUDCzO_AfJ4CyE2gZtYyVBngHoldClPwj6ihQdnRLl9z0aJHqprZuZU4g1Ddo0o8uNHeoQ%3D%3D)**

Thank you for helping be part of the solution during these challenges times – hunkering down, but not slowing down. And we’ll continue to do our work to keep you updated! Thanks, -Your Holcomb Crew |

name: Hickenlooper HQ
subject: Honesty
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado
body:
|
#
Folks, Here's the honest truth we want to share with you: We had set some ambitious online fundraising goals for March -- enough, in our estimation, to fight back against the GOP's attacks and support our grassroots organizing to win this election. But our fundraising numbers have slowed down quite a bit, for completely understandable reasons, and now we're off-course. On top of that, we did the right thing and canceled our in-person fundraising events, too. So, we're at-risk of falling short on our goals ahead of next week's FEC fundraising deadline -- and we need your help. **Can you add a donation to our campaign before our filing deadline on Tuesday? We only want you to pitch in if you can afford to at this time. We understand if not, but anything you can contribute today to help us flip this seat will go a long way.** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jwh-em-200326-truth?refcode=em-200326-full&amount=10&express_lane=true&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

[CHIP IN $25 NOW](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jwh-em-200326-truth?refcode=em-200326-full&amount=25&express_lane=true&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

---
[CHIP IN $100 NOW](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jwh-em-200326-truth?refcode=em-200326-full&amount=100&express_lane=true&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

| [CHIP IN $250 NOW](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jwh-em-200326-truth?refcode=em-200326-full&amount=250&express_lane=true&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
---
[ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jwh-em-200326-truth?refcode=em-200326-full&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
---
We're confident we can get our momentum back if enough of our supporters like you step up. But it's okay if you can't afford to donate today. A lot of families are facing financial hardship right now, and the most important thing you can do is take care of yourself and your loved ones.
Thanks for everything.
-- Team Hick
P.S. We know this is a difficult time, so if you'd like to pause our emails until this public health emergency has passed, [fill out this form](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/637710c4/691c9bd/686b8c7b/11a7597f/568593812/VEsB/?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "http://jhfp.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/covid-19?source=em200326").

name: Team Hagerty
subject: How you can help the coronavirus relief effort
body:
We wanted to share these important relief efforts with you
Peter,Team Hagerty is going to do everything we can to help our fellow Tennesseans - especially those who are vulnerable and at high risk for coronavirus. If you know someone who needs help getting a meal or supplies, email us: TNtogether@teamhagerty.com.We've also heard from a number of folks who want to help their neighbors during this trying time. We've compiled [a list of ways to help here.](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-nkyhuz-fuutjhhtj-r/)You should always check with these organizations before volunteering, but of course, if you are at high-risk, are sick, think you may be sick, or you have been around anyone who is sick, you should stay at home. In order to prevent the spread of the coronavirus, we must follow the guidance of President Trump, Governor Lee, and [the CDC.](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-nkyhuz-fuutjhhtj-y/) We're going to get through this together.Team Hagerty | [](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-nkyhuz-fuutjhhtj-j/)| [](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-nkyhuz-fuutjhhtj-t/)| [](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-nkyhuz-fuutjhhtj-i/)| [](https://hfs.cmail20.com/t/t-l-nkyhuz-fuutjhhtj-d/)
---|---|---|---
Team Hagerty
4515 Harding Pike
Suite 110
Nashville, TN 37205

name: Rick Kennedy
subject: Why Am I Fundraising In The Middle Of A Crisis?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rick Kennedy for Congress
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16567027/176124924/-1907457918?refcode=03042020_em_hdr&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWEyZTRkMi0zYzcwLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IUihifAsrU7L0mG4IVK2rhlU-q019q7hMofDYdK-XQk=)
---
Dear Friend, First and foremost, I hope this letter finds you and yours safe, healthy, and in the best possible spirits given the circumstances. I stepped back and consulted with my core team over the last week to reassess my campaign in light of the COVID-19 pandemic. We debated the ethics of fundraising in the current environment and whether we should continue, go full stop, or somewhere in between. I have decided not only to continue, but to go as hard as possible. This election is no longer just about __who__ you want to represent you in Washington, it's about __what__ you want the federal government to do and __how__ you want it to operate. It's too important for anything but an all-out effort. This administration, and many members of Congress, do not base their decisions on science, facts, or even the observable reality around us. The result is a complete lack of preparedness for, and utter failure to competently deal not only with this crisis, but just about every other area of governance. The only thing that will change the behavior in Washington is you, the voters. When you support candidates who pledge to engage in evidence-based decision making, to resume investing in America, to position us to be successful not just for the next election cycle, but for generations to come, and hold them accountable for their actions at the next election, only then will you get the government you deserve. I am that candidate. It's time for us all to stand up and say we've had enough. **[Let's make this happen together.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16567028/176124925/-1907457918?refcode=03272020_em_line&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWEyZTRkMi0zYzcwLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IUihifAsrU7L0mG4IVK2rhlU-q019q7hMofDYdK-XQk=) **Please dig deep and contribute what you can. | [$10](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16567029/176124926/1282640063?refcode=03272020_em_button&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWEyZTRkMi0zYzcwLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IUihifAsrU7L0mG4IVK2rhlU-q019q7hMofDYdK-XQk=) | [$25](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16567030/176124927/1282640063?refcode=03272020_em_button&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWEyZTRkMi0zYzcwLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IUihifAsrU7L0mG4IVK2rhlU-q019q7hMofDYdK-XQk=) | [$50](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16567031/176124928/1282640063?refcode=03272020_em_button&amount=50&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWEyZTRkMi0zYzcwLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IUihifAsrU7L0mG4IVK2rhlU-q019q7hMofDYdK-XQk=)

name: Josh Hicks
subject: How our campaign is adapting, and how you can help
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
body:
Friends,
Less than two weeks ago, our campaign made the call to pause typical campaign activities, including postponing all in-person events.
But I want to let you know: Our efforts to flip this seat are more important than ever.
Our campaign has been built on getting out to events, meeting people, and raising grassroots donations [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_L1] . And we’re quickly adapting to our new reality of social distancing, supporting our friends and neighbors, and transitioning to a completely virtual campaign.
Our team pulled together a memo on the state of the race. I wanted to make sure our supporters knew that we were adapting, and still on track. Please read the memo below and, if you're able, consider donating to our campaign before our first FEC deadline [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_btn1] of 2020 by Tuesday, March 31 at midnight.
Thank you,
Josh Hicks
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_btn1]

From : Brett Anders
To : Josh Hicks for Congress donors
Re : How our campaign is adapting, and how you can help
Our path to victory
First, we must perform well where Democrats succeeded in 2018 and 2019. That means maintaining 55-65% of the vote in Fayette and Franklin Counties (the cities of Lexington and Frankfort, respectively) by engaging voters who aren’t always engaged, voter registration, neighbor-to-neighbor voter contact, and a strong GOTV program. Second, we need to drive up the vote share in rural counties to 30-40% by winning swing voters and inconsistent voters who feel that Josh’s story resonates with them.
Fundraising
We launched the campaign on August 1 — part way through the third quarter of 2019. In just 60 days, we raised $120,000, and we closed 2019 out strong, raising more than $260,000 in Q4.
In our first quarter of 2020, we were on pace to increase our fundraising from the previous quarter. As of March 10, we’d already raised $200,000, with just $100,000 to go toward our quarterly goal of $300,000.
http://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_img1 [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_img1]
Our campaign’s growth, quarter over quarter, has been strong — and others are taking notice. We received endorsements from End Citizens United, an organization dedicated to campaign finance reform, and Serve America, Rep. Seth Moulton’s leadership organization dedicated to electing military veterans and civil servants.
http://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_img2 [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_img2]How we’re adapting now
A few weeks ago, our team made the decision to follow the guidelines set forth by Governor Andy Beshear and transition our campaign to work remotely.
That said, our team is continuing to work hard to elect Josh. In particular, I want to highlight three ways we’re adapting our campaign during the COVID-19 public health crisis:
Communicating with supporters virtually. We have moved as many events as possible to virtual events — through Zoom, Google Hangouts, and teleconferences — to ensure Josh can continue listening to Kentucky voters, centering their needs in this moment of crisis, and fighting for solutions together.

Our Team Hicks staff call Tuesday night

Fundraising. Over the last few weeks, our online fundraising efforts have shifted toward raising grassroots donations for local, community organizations. We've supported organizations like God's Pantry and Blue Grass Community Foundation who will help provide meal programs and support those in economically depressed areas hit hardest.
Building a volunteer base. We’re continuing to keep in touch with our volunteers through virtual channels (Facebook Groups, Zoom calls, etc.) so they can assist with our virtual outreach, recruit new volunteers, and build the support we need to win in November. Our phone-banking efforts, particularly focused on youth outreach and minority engagement, will be more important than ever.
How supporters can help
This crisis unfortunately underscores the importance of many of Josh’s beliefs and positions. Our economy was in crisis. Our healthcare system was in crisis. Low-wage workers and small businesses were living on the margins.
The most important thing our supporters can do is help us close the quarter strong. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_L2] Though many events are moving online or being postponed, the FEC deadline on March 31 remains. We were on track for a record quarter, and we’re keeping our eye on the ball.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_L4 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_L4]
In the coming weeks, we’ll continue to assess the following next steps in our campaign in light of the COVID-19 health crisis, adapting as needed:
- Campus organizing
- Faith outreach
- Digital and television advertising
- Voter contact
Our campaign will not turn to corporate donors or special interests — even if it means we have to work that much harder to continue growing our grassroots base as we approach the general election.
We’re still on track to not just be competitive, but win. Showing a strong quarter will allow us to remain flexible, adapt to the crisis, and ensure we send a champion for Central and Eastern Kentucky to Washington this November.
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_btn2]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_ftr1 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_ftr1]
Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
Josh Hicks for Congress
PO Box 1214
Lexington, KY 40588
United States
Josh Hicks is a Democrat running to represent Kentucky’s 6th Congressional District. He will be a representative who puts the needs of his constituents front and center in Washington. Josh grew up in Fleming County before leaving to serve in the Marines. After two deployments, he returned to Kentucky to serve his community as a police officer and attorney.
He’s running to represent hardworking, everyday Kentuckians. Not corporations. That means his campaign is relying on grassroots donations — lots of small donations from emails like this — not big checks from corporate special interests. Please consider making a contribution to Josh’s campaign today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_ftr2]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-mar-eoq?refcode=20200327_em_fr_memo_ftr3]

This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com

name: Corky Messner
subject: Shaheen’s war chest
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MESSNER FOR SENATE
body:
We must ramp up... help us kick off this critical effort! Read more >>
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/b871981973474402/b3c2a9ab1d41769ac3d6b838967d376c/33153) Peter, I just got off a long phone call with my team.

We’ve developed a comprehensive campaign plan for the next 90 days, taking into account the challenges we’re all dealing with given the COVID-19 outbreak and the shift in our tactics. We also went over our numbers.

_We need to raise $27,390 before we close out the quarter on March 31st!_

It’s vital that we post strong numbers. We need a strong show of force, and we MUST be able to fully fund our new 90-day plan. **[>> Please make a $5, $10, $25, $50, or $100 contribution to help! <<](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/b871981973474402/3da1d87037c4d720c3d6b838967d376c/33153)** Jeanne Shaheen is out-of-touch with New Hampshire voters and politically vulnerable... but she’s sitting on a MASSIVE $5.7 million campaign war chest, and will have the support of well-funded left-wing and pro-abortion groups to prop her up.

We’re trailing behind, plain and simple.

**If we don't step up our fundraising, I’m afraid that we will not be able to catch up, score a decisive primary victory, and defeat Jeanne Shaheen this November!**

clean_body: We must ramp up... help us kick off this critical effort! Read more >> Peter, I just got off a long phone call with my team. We’ve developed a comprehensive campaign plan for the next 90 days, taking into account the challenges we’re all dealing with given the COVID-19 outbreak and the shift in our tactics. We also went over our numbers. We need to raise $27,390 before we close out the quarter on March 31st! It’s vital that we post strong numbers. We need a strong show of force, and we MUST be able to fully fund our new 90-day plan. Jeanne Shaheen is out-of-touch with New Hampshire voters and politically vulnerable... but she’s sitting on a MASSIVE $5.7 million campaign war chest, and will have the support of well-funded left-wing and pro-abortion groups to prop her up. We’re trailing behind, plain and simple. If we don't step up our fundraising, I’m afraid that we will not be able to catch up, score a decisive primary victory, and defeat Jeanne Shaheen this November! There’s no time to waste. Our efforts NOW will determine the outcome on Election Day. . \- Corky | | | ---|--- id:2020-03-13-16:25:15:841t PAID FOR BY MESSNER FOR SENATE

| | | **How will you get involved in the campaign?**
* Attending virtual Organizing Bootcamp

* Making phone calls to voters
* Attending online events
* Sharing online events
* Reaching out to friends and family about MJ
* Donating online

Peter -- since the COVID-19 pandemic has forced us to put a pause on all in-person events, we're getting creative in spreading MJ's message all over the state -- and digital organizing is a huge part of that.

Now may not be the time for big rallies or door-to-door canvassing, but we still need your help to build our grassroots movement. With the runoff coming up we need to reach out to undecided voters about why MJ is the best candidate to take on Sen. Cornyn in November.

**[So tell us: How will you get involved?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903fb/7f51f7bc/4cce17fc/4ef1d906/4070663001/VEsF/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://mjfortexas.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/mj-involvementsurvey")**

\-- Team MJ

**[**DONATE**](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903fb/7f51f7bc/4cce17fc/4ef1d905/4070663001/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjExMjMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200327.1__1123__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

|
---

| It's clear we need a real leader for Texas and a Senate that prioritizes our health care. **[More than ever, we're relying on online fundraising to keep building the campaign we need to win in November, and we greatly appreciate your continued support>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903fb/7f51f7bc/4cce17fc/4ef1d904/4070663001/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjExMjMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-covidfooter?refcode=1123&amount=10&recurring=true")**

However, we know this is not an easy time for many. If you'd like to pause receiving fundraising emails for the next few weeks, **[please let us know here](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648903fb/7f51f7bc/4cce17fc/4ef1d97b/4070663001/VEsA/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/s/mj-covid-fewer-emails")**. We'll still keep in touch with important information and to ask your thoughts from time to time.

body:
There are a couple of things we don’t have on our campaign. We don’t have corporate PACs to call and ask for $5,000 checks. We don’t have millions of dollars to self-fund our campaign. But we do have a grassroots movement ready to get to work. | | | [
](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3wA/ni0YAA/t.30f/DWb9DCglTEaOFuX-Vpcenw/h0/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNKVPB7luivKSYF3y5kvWOhn6zH3WcP9C26HfSLQsmbil-2FjMr7UitirvbCBClBQjKwwkZqNsbTebQrNMMo-2BSRD0-2BoVJGPNi8Is1rqBf-2FdXhYrMWiYeimOVAA-2FmFSBUrZ2kb9L99ruwQkal7Swf-2Fi7T6IxhO1UK-2B9su-2BgLIgA-2F2stPIzRJEsD0GH5fhXB-2BqygP8RN-2BiiR1DryDi4aZlpCe1mEpmwqz9nAg8nrNM2so-2BbSnrUVeparNF-2FSqdM9YBTo8Us-3D/EyGq) Friend, **** **** **** There are a couple of things we don’t have on our campaign. We don’t have corporate PACs to call and ask for $5,000 checks. We don’t have millions of dollars to self-fund our campaign. But we do have a grassroots movement ready to get to work.
We’re going to show you what that looks like, but first, **_[can you support this grassroots movement with a donation today?](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3wA/ni0YAA/t.30f/DWb9DCglTEaOFuX-Vpcenw/h1/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNKVPB7luivKSYF3y5kvWOhn6zH3WcP9C26HfSLQsmbil2oHILHWfdTntqVNqyjVwip8bt55YwCgdTM8DmZVestQxKfcYObH3E5EAp50FHJVSoN5Gq8q8Z7esOkm49IgtidXN4RGGaMQ3W4A3mjolibRPOKfsYWkXf95WGEjNdW7EHyae1Mn9WtkuUBh7k-2BNzy-2BwbsvDI-2FRnOJMVJoZWNWsX5yW-2FDAMW8RGBIuvUrQ-2FjgIeFRIJ-2FPPMt4-2Bts1f3ESWU-3D/OpMw)_** We need organizers to train our volunteers, and texting and dialing platforms for them to use, and all of that is expensive. **_[We are $14,725 away from our end-of-quarter fundraising goal. Will you chip in before March 31st to fund our digital organizing program?](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3wA/ni0YAA/t.30f/DWb9DCglTEaOFuX-Vpcenw/h2/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNKVPB7luivKSYF3y5kvWOhn6zH3WcP9C26HfSLQsmbil4Y-2BT9VZeZ818WoPUa-2Ff0ZgJLnm9Yt-2B0Bnm3btnid81RydBNq7cU2iAgxG5j6xmiPxy-2F-2FHEXksYjP1QWpdVpVwtCELNU47SYPUkia4YJGm9CVc8LEBmy7bvvMWwnpSyKUUd44fCOpyTgE1TiWKHpPjt9twcFUAwNwdHz8LvNvT9ETf-2BNUHdkXRFknjq9oRvlMq-2FP1DsleWIfFXM-2B84mci-2BY-3D/wtgY)_**
There are lots of college and high school students home right now, and they are ready to get to work for Team Mondaire. Shout out to Sophie, an American University student and Pleasantville High School grad, who recruited seven friends to volunteer. They hung out on Zoom while making hundreds of calls the other evening!
[](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3wA/ni0YAA/t.30f/DWb9DCglTEaOFuX-Vpcenw/h3/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNKVPB7luivKSYF3y5kvWOhn6zH3WcP9C26HfSLQsmbil6HOHOyUeaj2ZgKax1wn7rCbTx4vVJEb1gklY0a5Ew-2BCsQPV5eSS-2FyX0NlUW5eBdHDMKMylGtZQagOe1PhR14uQZFVQ97Mbwgv4oxESpDqO6FrGYDM6fpsBMKAmnBX5eTPrMsPIPk0A5Cx9y4fZVO9Dqx-2BWozg690j7DKxMXpRlJ6vYE9Kkkq1p1swJ26vmpiENf6i41kvkgmFbI7BMHf5M-3D/X5TT)
Our new Digital Organizing Director, Maya, is already crushing it. She put together a COVID response program so our volunteers can reach out to vulnerable people in their community to see if they need any help. She’s also been organizing volunteer training to teach our new volunteers how to phone bank!
[](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3wA/ni0YAA/t.30f/DWb9DCglTEaOFuX-Vpcenw/h4/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNKVPB7luivKSYF3y5kvWOhn6zH3WcP9C26HfSLQsmbil-2FR67rFYTScQyZ6B4RNfVMqWjwWxb8YWwr06ZLMp2GRMswMrghnJ-2FMn0A8ZLAfF5NyIELllQ2E6sk4o4xcaDNoRdZT8Npb2p-2FvguywxMzg95mklMamJ-2F5IYWNrnaDJ2ypHjLd9GHVkWVWxesdLMyKfB-2BRgbmx0SUGM6RYM-2F-2BEqLuinS0ngKcw-2B57dCa4oc7GHl6-2Bw3RwNFH4zoeE9rCSjF8-3D/d5HD)
Rockland United, a local Indivisible chapter and amazing grassroots group, endorsed our campaign last month and just hosted a virtual meet and greet for us last night. Between Zoom and Facebook Live, over 80 people tuned in to hear from Mondaire, ask questions, and learn how to get involved. Special thanks to Matt and Meredith for helping organize the event!
[](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3wA/ni0YAA/t.30f/DWb9DCglTEaOFuX-Vpcenw/h5/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNKVPB7luivKSYF3y5kvWOhn6zH3WcP9C26HfSLQsmbilxbKheK9Zg4MUECasvlo8rYB18tJnBRrPAQ-2BC4-2Fg5PjIwd8GC-2FH8cCInOMw-2F86b4gios-2Fva4zEe1qD5mwEIrYFZxIARbsyVc8CRIx1qAaS-2B5Krqa6NAuEEtUNwW-2FUrvav7pXm1uTsrDr40Sroy6PHA-2BQNrJLjmnLa3DaF1vJfBr22eD9ykyw9uFQx8WKqulSZqYS6m8yMp0gwRfMNNsN0Jg-3D/I9gL)
Times are tough right now, but our current situation shows just how important it is to elect leaders like Mondaire who will work to make sure no American has to live paycheck to paycheck. We have a ton of momentum, but we need to keep it up. **_[The end of the quarter is in just four days. We need your help - can you make a donation to help us hit our goal and finish this campaign strong?](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3wA/ni0YAA/t.30f/DWb9DCglTEaOFuX-Vpcenw/h6/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNKVPB7luivKSYF3y5kvWOhn6zH3WcP9C26HfSLQsmbilx-2Fp62BiJijRVqwcgutdUOAW6w1J-2FV7SmTnelAihCTsaCusLQDAK-2BMSD9ko5f-2FuUT3EQULmCj2ewjsh6K51fjfFA-2F5EYe4ujlSkY5HgUkLsJ6Cl0yPiFrovj4BozD2X4kSu-2B0IQKMVIqpjoUYv8YQQj-2FPRjJhZZf-2FWK3zRZeCRI-2F4C1UdWLXg4tEXsTKhiQIr-2BkekmWazR5xcR7Hxvrq4Ts-3D/IomY)_**
Thank you!
Team Mondaire [Donate >>](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/3wA/ni0YAA/t.30f/DWb9DCglTEaOFuX-Vpcenw/h7/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNKVPB7luivKSYF3y5kvWOhn6zH3WcP9C26HfSLQsmbil4iL-2B3cOKlj8yMXAR64wjlxmhbvrSZxq6N8eu-2Fnt3ZKOkfYH85umZBjz8PgNmY08LrIgVrYl-2F0uvKCAolAO-2BfuhCEWbQ7ZnjUgyNU6HVPj8fVAbeMccC7s-2F48K3ufguM7ktyfgmlUAh-2F18hflI4Aqktt4ivSi1PrHPP1sCGyYBe-2FOvMA-2Bu-2BxhRX-2F3DIXGMvqLw0Hj3K-2F0eY6ePl5sOU0430-3D/vImk)

name: FiveThirtyEight Update (via Blue Georgia)
subject: MATCH alert!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warnock for Georgia
body:
| | |
---
**Friend: Nate Silver's FiveThirtyEight has now _confirmed_ that Senate control is up for grabs - and it could all come down to Georgia. **
That's why Mitch McConnell and Republicans are zeroing in on Georgia's Senate race - and why the GOP candidate has pledged to spend _$20 MILLION _of her personal fortune to buy this seat and strengthen McConnell's grip on power.
Reverend Raphael Warnock (D) is the strong leader Democrats need to win this race and take back the Senate - but Republicans are pulling out all the stops to defeat him. Simply put, they're _terrified_ that McConnell's majority is in serious danger.
**That's why this news couldn't have come at a better time:** **A generous group of donors is MATCHING ALL GIFTS, up to our crucial $50,000 end-of-quarter goal, to flip Georgia blue and take back the Senate for Democrats.**

Kathy is committed to being a champion for all North Carolinians and fighting for what we need most. As a community leader for more than 30 years, Kathy has the experience to bring people together to get things done in Washington.
We're proud to fight alongside EMILY's List to send strong leaders like Kathy to Congress to protect women's health care, reproductive rights, and so much more, and we hope that you'll join us in the fight, too.
[_**While we understand that not everyone is able to make campaign contributions at the moment, if you are able, please consider chipping in to send Kathy to Congress.**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d37e/3d199a51/2f61e329/7364f16b/3082453418/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIxNzQyNiIsImUzdHpiSFZuZlgwPSI6ImttZTIwMDMyNyIsImUzdDJaWEp6YVc5dWZYMD0iOiJhIn0%3D/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kme200327?refcode=kme200327_a_other&refcode2=17426")

name: Colin Lauderdale, Campaign Manager
subject: Only thing missing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
Mitch is running scared. He knows that our progressive movement is rising but there’s only one thing not falling into place:
#
_Our hearts go out to anyone struggling right now to make ends meet. Please take care of yourself — we only ask you to donate if you can afford to. Your support already means a lot to us._
* * *
Mitch is running scared. He knows that our progressive movement is rising, but there’s only one thing not falling into place:
**✅National media is reporting that a progressive fighter like Charles could be the key to taking down Mitch.** Policies that Charles is advocating for like Medicare for All, a Green New Deal, and free public college have record support.
✅**Political experts are saying that Democrats have a growing chance at winning back the Senate.** Americans are sick & tired of the daily dysfunction we witness from Mitch’s Republican Senate.
✅**Our campaign just received another endorsement today from the Income Movement.** They agree that Charles is “the best candidate to defeat Mitch McConnell” and to lead the fight to end generational poverty.
**✖****But the only thing that’s missing right now is our fundraising.** Our numbers dipped yesterday and we can’t afford to miss our upcoming $20,000 end-of-quarter goal.
**[We can have all the other pieces. But if our fundraising falls short especially as we reach an urgent FEC deadline, this can halt our momentum. Will you chip in $5 to help us get back on track?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16576566/176210637/-1771280062?refcode=em20200327&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNWY4Y2U2ZS01YTcwLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VtQVwovKM69R8cu_SNCdPOUrnIOgYIjg35ZgNulI-Wo=)**

We know that Charles has the strongest platform to inspire Kentuckians to vote for change, but without the resources we can’t spread his message.
We’re relying on donations from emails like this so we can keep our grassroots movement powered.
Time is ticking and we need all the help we can get before our big FEC fundraising deadline.
[**Even just giving $5 makes a huge difference as we fight to raise $20,000 by next Tuesday. Your support is appreciated in a critical moment like this for our campaign.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16576568/176210639/-1771280062?refcode=em20200327&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNWY4Y2U2ZS01YTcwLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VtQVwovKM69R8cu_SNCdPOUrnIOgYIjg35ZgNulI-Wo=)

name: Mary Mancini
subject: TNDP Weekly Recap: Even When Social Distancing, Democrats Work For The People
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC LEGISLATORS
SEND LETTER TO BILL LEE
After Wednesday morning reports announced over 700 cases of the deadly coronavirus in Tennessee and data suggested it's going to get worse, Tennessee Senate Democratic Caucus and Tennessee House Democratic Caucus united to write a letter to Bill Lee. The letter demanded transparency and a clear effective plan of action to protect Tennessee families. Read the full letter now! [https://www.facebook.com/tndem/posts/10157760267445831]
CELEBRATING ACA
10 YEAR ANNIVERSARY
This week marked the 10th Anniversary of Barack Obama signing the Affordable Care Act into law. Even though he has tried and failed to repeal it, Donald J. Trump relies heavily on ACA especially during this pandemic. Here's a quick list of some of the ways ACA is saving lives and protecting Americans:
*
Essential
Health
Benefits:
prescription
drugs
and
hospitalization.
Coronavirus
testing
and
treatment
are
essential
health
benefits
and
must
be
covered
by
all
ACA-compliant
plans.
*
Preexisting
Conditions:
anyone
who
contracts
COVID-19
won't
be
subject
to
future
discrimination.
*
Expansion:
more
than
20
million
Americans
gained
health
coverage.
People
with
insurance
are
much
more
likely
to
see
a
doctor
when
they
are
sick
and
to
get
the
treatment
they
need.
*
Created
CDC
Prevention
and
Public
Health
Fund:
helps
states
prepare
for
disease
outbreaks.
*
Key
Support
for
Rural
Hospitals:
between
2013
and
2015,
hospitals’
uncompensated
care
costs
decreased
by
$12
billion,
or
roughly
30
percent.
As
hospitals
prepare
for
an
influx
in
coronavirus
patients,
it
is
critical
that
they
are
paid
for
the
treatment
they
provide.
The ACA’s impact is prevalent now more than ever. We applaud you for your work in saving American lives not just during your term, but, for decades to come after. Thank you Mr. President, Barack Obama
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/maxresdefault.jpg]
CONVERSATIONS AND QUESTIONS:
REPRESENTATIVE PARKINSON
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/Screen%20Shot%202020-03-27%20at%2010.49.22%20AM.png]
We sat down with State Rep. Antonio Parkinson to discuss the state legislature, the coronavirus in Tennessee, and this year's upcoming elections!
Watch now! [https://www.facebook.com/tndem/videos/2758698511024385/]
Conversations and Questions is a weekly happy hour where we sit down with candidates on the 2020 ballot to discuss current issues, their platform, and their campaignsKicking us off this week, we sat down with Representative Antonio Parkinson to discuss the current state of Tennessee’s legislature and where we are beginning this campaign year.
HISTORIC REMOTE CONGRESSIONAL
CONVENTIONS
In an historic election year, over 1500 Democrats across the state participated by phone in Tennessee Democratic Party Congressional District Conventions. On short notice due to the Coronavirus Pandemic, and in partnership with the DNC, the Tennessee Democratic Party quickly organized 24 seperate calls all on one day with over 1500 people participating.
Each Congressional District Conventions was attended by county selectors who joined together to elect their Congressional District Delegates to the DNC Convention in Milwaukee this summer. In total, 42 of the state’s 73 delegates were elected this past Saturday.
“Tennessee Democrats rallied in great numbers to participate in this important process. We saw the hunger for change on Super Tuesday, during the County Conventions held two weeks ago, and now we’ve seen it during Congressional District Conventions. People are turning out in historic numbers to participate in our democracy and vote Trump out of office in November.” - Mary Mancini, TNDP Chair
IF YOU DON'T KNOW, NOW YOU KNOW:
A TNDP PODCAST
https://tndp.org/podcast/ [https://tndp.org/podcast/]
This year we head to the ballot box, both locally and nationally. It feels weird to say that given the current state of our country and world, but, elections now are crucial to our economy and health. Use some of your time at home to catch up on some of the biggest stories in Tennessee's legislature.
If You Don’t Know, Now You Know: A TNDP Podcast is hosted by Tennessee Democratic Party Chair, Mary Mancini. Recorded live from the Tennessee state capitol in Nashville, we speak with lawmakers, activists, and candidates, people who can tell you – and show you – how the laws are made that affect our lives. There’s no shortage of issues to be covered – about freedom, opportunity, equity, justice, and safety.
Listen now! [https://tndp.org/podcast/]
TWEETS OF THE WEEK
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/Screen%20Shot%202020-03-27%20at%2011.07.32%20AM.png][https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/Screen%20Shot%202020-03-27%20at%2011.08.04%20AM.png]
YOUR CONTRIBUTIONS IN ACTION
In the face of the pandemic, Democrats are leading the charge to save and protect Tennesseeans. Before they were elected though, they had to be recruited and trained through the Democratic Party. Any contribution you give today, will help us strengthen our pipeline of candidates and ensure we have effective campaigns running.
Will you consider donating today? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/oNKhpMgsTk-zA4pXE2fn4w2]
PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR. GEETA MCMILLAN, TREASURER NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. CONTRIBUTIONS OR GIFTS TO TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY ARE NOT TAX DEDUCTIBLE.
Tennessee Democratic Party
319 Plus Park Blvd
Nashville, TN 37217
United States

name: =?utf-8?Q?Suzanne=20LaTurner?=
subject: Looking on the bright side
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kansans for LaTurner
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/ad511c0d39e7/looking-on-the-bright-side?e=1050d051da)
---
| | Friend,

It has been an interesting two weeks in the LaTurner household, adjusting to a new schedule with our kids home full time. To be honest, while I wish the reason for this change was different, I love having my family all together at home.

I have noticed a greater appreciation for my home and the people in it as we spend this extra time together. Just for fun, I thought I might give you a glimpse into what we're up to and a few of our kids' silly antics from this week.

If you want to stay up to date on what is going on in our household, you can follow Jake on Facebook. [https://www.facebook.com/JakeLaTurnerKS/](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=43e9c75189&e=1050d051da)
---
|
---
Our kitchen island now doubles as a desk for school work. To all of you who are figuring out how to homeschool kids right now, I'd love to hear what is working for you. It is certainly a challenging learning curve and makes me even more grateful for the work our school teachers do each day.
|
---
Gus is our youngest and most mischievous. We discovered this week he's been bringing earthworms in to the house and keeping them in his pockets...
|
---
---
| Maggie and mom like to get creative and decided to make her some hairbows.
---
|
---
Joe gave Dad a hand fixing the dryer. He's a great helper and likes learning how things work.
|
---
Our oldest daughter Ava received this sweet card from one of her school teachers. It is heart warming to see this kind of thoughtfulness being shown to my kids by such caring individuals. A big thank you to all who have reached out to our family to see how we are doing. It means a lot.
| [ ](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=cb18cb4350&e=1050d051da)
---
Here's one final clip of Gus and Jake doing his silly version of "Old McDonald". We learn new things from our kids every day. :-)
| Our family prays daily for everyone to stay safe and healthy. My deepest sympathies to those who have family members or friends who are suffering because of COVID-19 and the disruptions it has caused in so many different areas of life right now.

May God bless you and your loved ones.

Suzanne LaTurner
---
|
---
---

These policies would destroy our district and Arizona as a whole.
**We cannot afford a president that would** **undo ****all of our progress. **
Tiffany Shedd is running for Congress to support President Trump. clean_body: Friends, Likely Democratic presidential nominee, Joe Biden, claims that he’s a moderate . . . but he actually supports open borders, free healthcare for illegal immigrants, getting rid of ICE, and sanctuary cities. Friend, that’s far from moderate. In fact, that’s incredibly progressive. These policies would destroy our district and Arizona as a whole. We cannot afford a president that would undo all of our progress. Tiffany Shedd is running for Congress to support President Trump. She wants to build the wall, protect our border, and support our law enforcement officers. Our FEC deadline is Tuesday night and we have to reach it to send a message to Biden and the radical Democrats that Arizona does not support his dangerous agenda → We know this is a difficult time, but any help would be appreciated. All donations DOUBLED Friend, keep Biden’s radical agenda out of Arizona → Thank you for your support, Team Shedd Paid for by Tiffany Shedd for Congress Committee

| Friends, As you know, Governor Sununu declared a Stay at Home Emergency order effective at midnight tonight. Below find a list of essential services that will remain open during this order. We hope that you all stay safe and healthy during this difficult time. \- The New Hampshire Senate Republicans
---
---
| |
---
|
---
| |
---
| **Law Enforcement, Public Safety, First Responders**
* Personnel in emergency management, law enforcement, Emergency Management Systems, fire, and corrections, including front line and management required to maintain operations
* Emergency Medical Technicians
* 911 call center employees, including telecommunicators, dispatchers and managers
* Information and Analysis Center employees
* Hazardous material responders from government and the private sector.
* Workers – including contracted vendors -- who maintain digital systems infrastructuresupporting law enforcement and emergency service operations.**Food and Agriculture**

* Workers supporting groceries, pharmacies, florists, and other retail, including farmers markets and farm stands, that sells food and beverage products, including liquor stores
* Restaurant carry-out and quick serve food operations, including beer and wine curbside and takeout - Carry-out and delivery food employees
* Food manufacturer employees and their supplier employees—to include those employed in food processing facilities; livestock, poultry, seafood slaughter facilities; pet and animal feed processing facilities; human food facilities producing by-products for animal food; beverage production facilities, including breweries, wineries, and distilleries; and the production of food packaging
* Farm workers to include those employed in animal food, feed, and ingredient production, packaging, distribution, and retail; manufacturing, packaging, and distribution of veterinary drugs; truck delivery and transport; farm and fishery labor needed to produce our food supply domestically
* Farm workers and support service workers to include those who field crops, beekeeping; commodity inspection; fuel ethanol facilities; storage facilities; and other agricultural inputs
* Workers supporting the seafood and fishing industry
* Commercial and residential landscaping services, including golf courses.
* Employees and firms supporting food, feed, and beverage distribution, includingwarehouse workers, vendor-managed inventory controllers and blockchain managers
* Workers supporting the sanitation of all food manufacturing processes and operationsfrom wholesale to retail
* Company cafeterias - in-plant cafeterias used to feed employees; food service workers inresidential schools with students who are unable to leave campus
* Workers in food testing labs in private industries and in institutions of higher education
* Food banks
* Nurseries, greenhouses, garden centers, and agriculture supply stores
* Workers essential for assistance programs and government payments
* Employees of companies engaged in the production of chemicals, medicines, vaccines,and other substances used by the food and agriculture industry, including pesticides, herbicides, fertilizers, minerals, enrichments, and other agricultural production aids

* Animal agriculture workers to include those employed in veterinary health; manufacturing and distribution of animal medical materials, animal vaccines, animal drugs, feed ingredients, feed, and bedding, etc.; transportation of live animals, animal medical materials; transportation of deceased animals for disposal; raising of animals for food; animal production operations; slaughter and packing plants and associated regulatory and government workforce
* Organizations and workers responsible for the care and custody of animals, pets and livestock
* Workers who support the manufacture and distribution of forest products, including, but not limited to timber, paper, and other wood products
* Employees engaged in the manufacture and maintenance of equipment and other infrastructure necessary to agricultural production and distribution**Health Care/ Public Health / Human Services**

* Workers providing COVID-19 testing; Workers that perform critical clinical research needed for COVID-19 response
* Medical Professionals and caregivers (e.g., physicians, dentists, psychologists, mid-level practitioners, nurses and assistants, infection control and quality assurance personnel, pharmacists, physical and occupational therapists and assistants, social workers, speech pathologists and diagnostic and therapeutic technicians and technologists, other providers of mental and behavioral health care, personal care attendants, home health aides and home care workers)
* Hospital and laboratory personnel (including accounting, administrative, admitting and discharge, engineering, epidemiological, source plasma and blood donation, food service, housekeeping, medical records, information technology and operational technology, nutritionists, sanitarians, respiratory therapists, etc.)
* Workers in other medical facilities (including Ambulatory Health and Surgical, Blood Banks, Medical Clinics, Community Mental Health Centers, Methadone/OBOT Clinics, 24 hour Diversionary and Residential Behavioral Health Providers, Comprehensive Outpatient rehabilitation, End Stage Renal Disease, Health Departments, Home Health care, Hospices, Hospitals, Nursing Facilities, Rest Homes, Assisted Living Residences, Organ Pharmacies, Procurement Organizations, Psychiatric Residential, Residential Treatment Schools, Rural Health Clinics and Federally Qualified Health Center s and Community Health Centers, State Hospitals)
* Workers in other 24/7 community resident services serving children and youth, and individuals with developmental, intellectual, physical and/or cognitive disabilities
* Workers in recovery centers and sober homes
* Manufacturers, technicians, logistics and warehouse operators, and distributors of ornecessary to the supply chain of medical equipment, personal protective equipment (PPE), medical gases, pharmaceuticals, blood and blood products, vaccines, testing materials, laboratory supplies, cleaning, sanitizing, disinfecting or sterilization supplies, and tissue and paper towel products
* Public health / community health workers, including those who compile, model, analyze and communicate public health information

* Blood and plasma donors and the employees of the organizations that operate and manage related activities
* Workers that manage health plans, billing, and health information, who cannot practically work remotely
* Workers who conduct community-based public health functions, conducting epidemiologic surveillance, compiling, analyzing and communicating public health information, who cannot practically work remotely
* Workers performing cybersecurity functions at healthcare and public health facilities, who cannot practically work remotely
* Workers conducting research critical to COVID-19 response
* Workers performing security, incident management, and emergency operations functionsat or on behalf of healthcare entities including healthcare coalitions, who cannotpractically work remotely
* Workers who support food, shelter, and social services, and other necessities of life foreconomically disadvantaged or otherwise needy individuals, such as those residing inshelters
* Pharmacy employees necessary for filling prescriptions
* Workers performing mortuary services and workers at funeral homes, crematoriums, andcemeteries
* Workers who coordinate with other organizations to ensure the proper recovery,handling, identification, transportation, tracking, storage, and disposal of human remains and personal effects; certify cause of death; and facilitate access to mental/behavioral health services to the family members, responders, and survivors of an incident**Energy
Electricity industry:**

* Workers who maintain, ensure, or restore the reliable generation, transmission, and distribution of electric power, including call centers, utility workers, reliability engineers and fleet maintenance technicians
* Workers needed for safe and secure operations at nuclear generation
* Workers at generation, transmission and electric blackstart facilities
* Workers at Reliability Coordinator (RC), Balancing Authorities (BA), and primary andbackup Control Centers (CC), including but not limited to independent system operators,regional transmission organizations, and balancing authorities
* Mutual assistance personnel
* IT and OT technology staff – for EMS (Energy Management Systems) and SupervisoryControl and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems, and utility data centers; Cybersecurityengineers; cybersecurity risk management
* Vegetation management crews and traffic workers who support
* Environmental remediation/monitoring technicians
* Instrumentation, protection, and control technicians

**Petroleum workers:**
* Petroleum product storage, pipeline, marine transport, terminals, rail transport, road transport
* Crude oil storage facilities, pipeline, and marine transport
* Petroleum refinery facilities
* Petroleum security operations center employees and workers who support emergencyresponse services
* Petroleum operations control rooms/centers
* Petroleum drilling, extraction, production, processing, refining, terminal operations,transporting, and retail for use as end-use fuels or feedstocks for chemical manufacturing
* Onshore and offshore operations for maintenance and emergency response
* Retail fuel centers such as gas stations and truck stops, and the distribution systems thatsupport them**Natural and propane gas workers:**

* Natural gas transmission and distribution pipelines, including compressor stations, and road transport
* Underground storage of natural gas
* Natural gas processing plants, and those that deal with natural gas liquids
* Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) facilities
* Natural gas security operations center, natural gas operations dispatch and controlrooms/centers natural gas emergency response and customer emergencies, includingnatural gas leak calls
* Drilling, production, processing, refining, and transporting natural gas for use as end-usefuels, feedstocks for chemical manufacturing, or use in electricity generation
* Propane gas dispatch and control rooms and emergency response and customeremergencies, including propane leak calls
* Propane gas service maintenance and restoration, including call centers
* Processing, refining, and transporting natural liquids, including propane gas, for use asend-use fuels or feedstocks for chemical manufacturing
* Propane gas storage, transmission, and distribution centers**Steam workers:**

* Workers who support steam distribution companies’ provision of district heating and anyelectric generation
* Workers who support steam distribution companies’ dispatch and control rooms andemergency response and customer emergencies, including steam leak calls
* Workers who support steam distribution companies’ service maintenance and restoration,including call centers
* Workers who support steam distribution companies’ storage, transmission, anddistribution centers**Waste and Wastewater**

Employees needed to operate and maintain public and private drinking water and wastewater/drainage infrastructure, including:
* Operational staff at water authorities
* Operational staff at community water systems
* Operational staff at wastewater treatment facilities
* Workers repairing water and wastewater conveyances and performing required samplingor monitoring
* Operational staff for water distribution and testing
* Operational staff at wastewater collection facilities
* Operational staff and technical support for SCADA Control systems
* Chemical disinfectant suppliers for wastewater and personnel protection
* Workers that maintain digital systems infrastructure supporting water and wastewateroperations**Transportation and Logistics**

* Employees supporting or enabling transportation functions, including dispatchers, maintenance and repair technicians, warehouse workers, truck stop and rest area workers, and workers that maintain and inspect infrastructure (including those that require cross- border travel)
* Employees of firms providing services that enable logistics operations, including cooling, storing, packaging, and distributing products for wholesale or retail sale or use
* Mass transit workers, including contracted vendors providing transportation and maintenance services to public transit authorities
* Workers critical to operating rental car companies and Transportation Network Companies (TNCs) that facilitate continuity of operations for essential workforces, and other essential travel
* Workers responsible for operating dispatching passenger, commuter and freight trains and public transportation and buses and maintaining rail and transit infrastructure and equipment
* Maritime transportation workers - port workers, mariners, equipment operators
* Truck drivers who haul hazardous and waste materials to support critical infrastructure,capabilities, functions, and services
* Bicycle repair shops
* Automotive sales, repair and maintenance facilities
* Workers who respond to and clear traffic crashes, including contracted vendors anddispatchers
* Manufacturers and distributors (to include service centers and related operations) ofpackaging materials, pallets, crates, containers, and other supplies needed to supportmanufacturing, packaging staging and distribution operations
* Postal and shipping workers, to include private companies
* Workers who support moving and storage services
* Employees who repair and maintain vehicles, aircraft, rail equipment, marine vessels, andthe equipment and infrastructure that enables operations that encompass movement of cargo and passengers

* Air transportation employees, including air traffic controllers, ramp personnel, aviation security, and aviation management and other workers – including contracted vendors –providing services for air passengers
* Workers who support the maintenance and operation of cargo by air transportation, including flight crews, maintenance, airport operations, and other on- and off- airport facilities workers**Public Works**

* Workers who support the operation, inspection, and maintenance of essential dams, locks and levees
* Workers who support the operation, inspection, and maintenance of essential public works facilities and operations, including roads and bridges, water and sewer main breaks, fleet maintenance personnel, construction of critical or strategic infrastructure, traffic signal maintenance, emergency location services for buried utilities, maintenance of digital systems infrastructure supporting public works operations, and other emergent issues
* Workers – including contracted vendors – involved in the construction of critical or strategic infrastructure including public works construction, airport operations, water, sewer, gas, electrical, nuclear, oil refining and other critical energy services, roads and highways, public transportation, solid waste collection and removal, municipal transfer stations, and internet, and telecommunications systems (including the provision of essential global, national, and local infrastructure for computing services)
* Workers such as plumbers, electricians, exterminators, inspectors and other service providers who provide services that are necessary to maintaining the safety, sanitation, and essential operation of residences, construction sites and projects, and needed facilities
* Support, such as road and line clearing and utility relocation, to ensure the availability of needed facilities, transportation, energy and communications
* Support to ensure the effective removal, storage, and disposal of residential and commercial solid waste and hazardous waste
* Licensed site clean-up professionals and other workers addressing hazardous spills, waste sites, and remediation.
* Workers who support the operation, maintenance and public safety of state parks, forests, wildlife management areas, water supply protection lands, and other critical natural resources.
* Workers who support storm clean-up operations (e.g., foresters).**Communications and Information Technology****Communications:**

• Maintenance of communications infrastructure- including privately owned and maintained communication systems- supported by technicians, operators, call-centers, wireline and wireless providers, cable service providers, satellite operations, undersea cable landing stations, Internet Exchange Points, and manufacturers and distributors of communications equipment
* Workers who support radio, television, and media service, including, but not limited to front line news reporters, studio, and technicians for newsgathering and reporting
* Workers at Independent System Operators and Regional Transmission Organizations, and Network Operations staff, engineers and/or technicians to manage the network or operate facilities
* Engineers, technicians and associated personnel responsible for infrastructure construction and restoration, including contractors for construction and engineering of fiber optic cables
* Installation, maintenance and repair technicians that establish, support or repair service as needed
* Central office personnel to maintain and operate central office, data centers, and other network office facilities
* Customer service and support staff, including managed and professional services as well as remote providers of support to transitioning employees to set up and maintain home offices, who interface with customers to manage or support service environments and security issues, including payroll, billing, fraud, and troubleshooting
* Dispatchers involved with service repair and restoration**Information Technology:**

* Workers who support command centers, including, but not limited to Network Operations Command Center, Broadcast Operations Control Center and Security Operations Command Center
* Data center operators, including system administrators, HVAC & electrical engineers, security personnel, IT managers, data transfer solutions engineers, software and hardware engineers, and database administrators
* Client service centers, field engineers, and other technicians supporting critical infrastructure, as well as manufacturers and supply chain vendors that provide hardware and software, and information technology equipment (to include microelectronics and semiconductors) for critical infrastructure
* Workers responding to cyber incidents involving critical infrastructure, including medical facilities, SLTT governments and federal facilities, energy and utilities, and banks and financial institutions, and other critical infrastructure categories and personnel
* Workers supporting the provision of essential global, national and local infrastructure for computing services (incl. cloud computing services), business infrastructure, web-based services, and critical manufacturing
* Workers supporting communications systems and information technology used by law enforcement, public safety, medical, energy and other critical industries
* Support required for continuity of services, including janitorial/cleaning personnel**Other Community-Based Essential Functions**

• Workers to ensure continuity of building functions, including local and state inspectors and administrative support of inspection services who are responsible for the inspection of elevators, escalators, lifts, buildings, plumbing and gas fitting, electrical work, and other safety related professional work
* Security staff to maintain building access control and physical security measures
* Residential and commercial janitorial and cleaning services
* Elections personnel
* Trade Officials (FTA negotiators; international data flow administrators)
* Weather forecasters
* Workers that maintain digital systems infrastructure supporting other critical governmentoperations
* Workers at operations centers necessary to maintain other essential functions
* Workers who support necessary credentialing, vetting and licensing operations fortransportation workers including holders of Commercial Drivers Licenses
* Workers who are critical to facilitating trade in support of the national, state and localemergency response supply chain
* Educators and staff supporting public and private emergency childcare programs,including remote learning and facilitating distance learning among residential schools for students with disabilities, K-12 schools, colleges, and universities, provision of school meals, or performing other essential student support functions, if operating under rules for social distancing
* Workers at hotel and commercial lodging facilities
* Construction Workers who support the construction, operation, inspection, andmaintenance of construction sites and construction projects (including housingconstruction)
* Workers that provide services for or determine eligibility for public benefits such assubsidized health care, food and feeding programs, residential and congregate care programs, shelter, in-home supportive services, child welfare, juvenile justice programs, adult protective services and social services, and other necessities of life for economically disadvantaged or otherwise needy individuals (including family members)
* Professional services (such as legal and accounting services) and payroll and employee benefit services, when necessary to assist in compliance with legally mandated activities and critical sector services or where failure to provide such services during the time of the order would result in significant prejudice
* Commercial retail stores that supply essential sectors, including convenience stores, pet supply stores, auto supplies and repair, hardware and home improvement, and home appliance retailers
* Laundromats, dry cleaning, and laundry services
* Workers and instructors supporting academies and training facilities and courses for thepurpose of graduating students and cadets that comprise the essential workforce for allidentified critical sectors
* Workers at places of worship**Manufacturing**

• Manufacturing companies, distributors, and supply chain companies producing and supplying materials and products for industries that include, but are not limited to, pharmaceutical, technology, biotechnology, healthcare, chemicals and sanitization, waste pickup and disposal, agriculture, food and beverage, transportation, energy, steel and steel products, petroleum and fuel, construction, gun and related products (including associated retail), operations of dams, water and wastewater treatment, national defense, communications, as well as products used by other essential businesses and operations **Hazardous Materials**
* Workers at nuclear facilities, workers managing medical waste, workers managing waste from pharmaceuticals and medical material production, and workers at laboratories processing test kits
* Workers who support hazardous materials response and cleanup
* Workers who maintain digital systems infrastructure supporting hazardous materialsmanagement operations**Financial Services**

* Banks, financial services institutions, credit unions, insurance, payroll, regional development corporations, and accounting services
* Workers who are needed to process and maintain systems for processing financial transactions and services (e.g., payment, clearing, and settlement; wholesale funding; insurance services; and capital markets activities)
* Workers who are needed to provide consumer access to banking and lending services, including ATMs, and to move currency and payments (e.g., armored cash carriers)
* Workers who support financial operations, such as those staffing data and securityoperations centers**Chemical**

* Workers supporting the chemical and industrial gas supply chains, including workers at chemical manufacturing plants, workers in laboratories, workers at distribution facilities, workers who transport basic raw chemical materials to the producers of industrial and consumer goods, including hand sanitizers, food and food additives, pharmaceuticals, textiles, and paper products.
* Workers supporting the safe transportation of chemicals, including those supporting tank truck cleaning facilities and workers who manufacture packaging items
* Workers supporting the production of protective cleaning and medical solutions, personal protective equipment, and packaging that prevents the contamination of food, water, medicine, among others essential products
* Workers supporting the operation and maintenance of facilities (particularly those with high risk chemicals and/or sites that cannot be shut down) whose work cannot be done remotely and requires the presence of highly trained personnel to ensure safe operations, including plant contract workers who provide inspections
* Workers who support the production and transportation of chlorine and alkali manufacturing, single-use plastics, and packaging that prevents the contamination or supports the continued manufacture of food, water, medicine, and other essential products, including glass container manufacturing**Defense Industrial Base**

* Workers who support the essential services required to meet national security commitments to the federal government and U.S. Military. These individuals, include but are not limited to, aerospace; mechanical and software engineers, manufacturing/production workers; IT support; security staff; security personnel; intelligence support, aircraft and weapon system mechanics and maintainers
* Personnel working for companies, and their subcontractors, who perform under contract to the Department of Defense providing materials and services to the Department of Defense, and government-owned/contractor-operated and government- owned/government-operated facilities**Special Note** If the function of your business is not listed above, but you believe that it is essential or it is an entity providing essential services or functions, you may request designation as an essential business.Requests by businesses to be designated an essential function should only be made if they are NOT covered by the guidance.These requests should be submitted to [essential@nheconomy.com](mailto:essential@nheconomy.com) and include basic contact information and a brief justification.

name: Jason Yates
subject: COVID-19 Pandemic Survey - what do people of faith think?
body:
Peter,
The COVID-19 pandemic has dramatically affected nearly every American in the country. Amid all the changes and challenges, we are eager to learn what you think about this pandemic and its impact on your life and the United States.
Please take just a moment and answer these ten survey questions. It will take you less than five minutes, and it will help us tell the nation what people of faith think.
You can take the survey here: **[https://myfaithvotes.org/covid-19-pandemic-survey](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ibMzrge1zBfOv81lusjLGlBNT3IRn-QcmOx_I0KcW_xIPQqXuHO6thjSL6EZluwt4WD3DZQ6yXGjI7tAXSo4-ac&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200327srv01&n=1)**
I know you’re busy, but your voice is very important. Collectively we are working hard to ensure people of faith are heard at the national level.
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ibMzrge1zBfOv81lusjLGlBNT3IRn-QcmOx_I0KcW_xIPQqXuHO6thjSL6EZluwt4WD3DZQ6yXGjI7tAXSo4-ac&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200327srv01&n=2)
**United. We Stand.**
Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200327srv01&n=3)
©2020 My Faith Votes
10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com).
To stop receiving emails, [click here](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/unsubscribe?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200327srv01&n=4).

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Gallup Poll: Majority Of Americans Disapprove Of News Media Handling Of Wuhan Coronavirus
body:
"Of nine leaders and institutions rated by Americans in a new poll on their response to the novel Wuhan Coronavirus, the media fared the worst." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=827457d9fc&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Gallup Poll: Majority Of Americans Disapprove Of News Media Handling Of Wuhan Coronavirus**

“Of nine leaders and institutions rated by Americans in a new poll on their response to the novel Wuhan Coronavirus, the media fared the worst, and it’s not even close,” Tristan Justice reports for _The Federalist_.

“According to a new Gallup poll released Wednesday, the media was the only institution that scored a negative approval rating among the public with only 44 percent of Americans approving of the way the media has covered the virus.”

The President, Vice President, and Centers for Disease Control, by comparison, each earned at least 60 percent approval from Americans.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0e8eed8b5d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The director-general of the World Health Organization praised President Trump’s ‘political commitment’ to preventing the spread of coronavirus as the global community works to ‘suppress and control’ the pandemic,” Emily Jacobs reports for the [_New York Post_.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4df35d921b&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| Even though President Trump has been the most accessible President in history, the media has long pushed “for daily White House press conferences so that they could have the opportunity to be on camera and pressure the Trump administration.” They got their wish—but with the President’s approval numbers on the rise, now members of the press aren’t happy with the briefings, Mollie Hemingway writes in [_The Federalist_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dae49a0af2&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| Harvard University, one of the richest schools in America, “is cutting its subcontracted dining hall workers without pay as it shuts down in response to the coronavirus pandemic. The move is drawing criticism from employment rights advocates on and off campus,” Alana Goodman reports for [_The Washington Free Beacon_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3f92f98f0e&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5721beb8bb&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f90e3f84cc&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=d881fdb533)

name: Patty Judge
subject: Can you help out my friend Rita?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa
body:
| | | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/PjA2UoDWNEHsy4PqgoxPvzcV9cDnKqlNDP3X3R7Pf8o=/kcljrWuE0jMMyBbZ3lSyGuZ97BGZPOWpX33cpYxYyho=?refcode=200327-email) _We are grateful to have you on Team Hart! We know that right now many people are facing financial hardships, so if you cannot afford to give today -- that 's okay. We appreciate you opening and reading our campaign updates. If you would like to turn off these emails for a bit, then you can do so [here](https://act.myngp.com/el/PjA2UoDWNEHsy4PqgoxPvzcV9cDnKqlNDP3X3R7Pf8o=/zYPtWBxV_k5enwvf9JzdMAw21l7ER8FXuj0qXmlpOOM=)._
* * *
Peter I've known Rita for a long time; she lives our Iowa values. She listens to the people and with so much on the line in the 2020 election, we need someone like her advocating for us in Washington. She can win because she cares about the people of IA-02. She's a teacher who listens to everyone. name: =?utf-8?Q?Bobby=20Schilling?=
subject: Schilling for Life
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/bobbyforiowa.com/schilling-for-life?e=90fad69796)
---
| |
---
|
---
| |
# **Bobby Schilling is _Unapologetically_ _ PRO-LIFE!_**
I was asked recently why I'm running for US Congress, and why I decided to get into politics in the first place. The answer is simple, we need someone to protect life at the federal level. I have 10 kids with my wife Christie, and 13 grandkids, every life is precious and worth fighting for. To this day, I'm so very proud to have a 100% A+ rating from National Right to Life, and I will continue to fight for the unborn when I am elected in 2020. Our opponent Mariannette Miller-Meeks has never once been endorsed by National Right to Life and to this day has refused to fill out their official candidate questionnaire in all of her 4 failed attempts at this congressional seat.

NATIONAL RIGHT TO LIFE ENDORSEMENTS
* 2008
* Mariannette Miller-Meeks : **NO* **
* ***Mariannette Miller-Meeks NAMED "GREAT PRETENDER" BY IOWA RIGHT TO LIFE**
* 2010
* Bobby Schilling: **YES**
* Marianette Miller-Meeks: **NO**
* 2012
* Bobby Schilling: **YES**
* 2014
* Bobby Schilling: **YES**
* Marianette Miller-Meeks: **NO**

BOBBY SCHILLING: **100% Rating from NATIONAL RIGHT TO LIFE**
Mariannette Miller-Meeks: HAS NEVER FILLED OUT THE QUESTIONNAIRE

My friends, we need someone who is a **TRUE FIGHTER FOR THE UNBORN** , not a certified "great pretender". That's why I'm asking for your support in the Primary Election on June 2nd. **LETS WIN THIS THING!** For Iowa and for **LIFE** ,

\- Bobby Schilling
