name: =?utf-8?Q?Janet=20Barresi?=
subject: It's time for some good news
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://us20.campaign-archive.com/?e=795c6d9796&u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=8167b3f440)
---
| |
---
| | Peter, If it feels like all that you hear out of the news these days is negativity, you're not alone. I think it's time we have some good news.

**CARES Act**
Economic help is on the way. The Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act (CARES Act) has now passed both chambers of Congress and just signed into law by President Trump.

This legislation will provide desperately needed relief for Oklahoma workers and businesses struggling through this government-mandated shutdown. President Trump's leadership forged rarely seen bipartisan cooperation to help workers and small businesses. Together, we will defeat this virus, protect the most vulnerable and reopen businesses so Oklahoma can get back to work.

**More Testing**
A week after Governor Stitt said that our state was critically low on materials to test for COVID-19, we're reportedly going to be able to process 2,000 tests a day following Oklahoma State University receiving a shipment of 10,000 kits. Testing by OSU and the University of Oklahoma is expected to speed up results, which have been lagging for several days at some labs.

**The Power of Prayer**
The Governor declared a statewide Day of Prayer yesterday. Think about how beautiful that is. For one day, all Oklahomans united in prayer for healing and safety over our great state. Prayer is never a last resort. It is our first response.

_"Heal me, O Lord, and I will be healed; save me and I will be saved, for you are the one I praise."
\- Jeremiah 17:14_

I hope this was a reminder that we are all in this together and that we can get through this pandemic.

As always, I'm continuing to lift you and your family up in prayer.

Best,
\--Janet Barresi
---

name: Lexie, Team Christy
subject: Finance update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| []()
---

| | _The number one priority during this time is to make sure everyone helps mitigate the spread of COVID-19 in California and across the country. Our campaign is doing our part by canceling all in-person events, moving to a digital operation, and supporting local efforts to help those in need. Emails like this are a critical part of our ability to run a strong campaign that can win._

Peter,

We just learned Mike Garcia reserved nearly $68,000 in TV ads. Worse, we know it's just a drop in the bucket as to what his special interest backers are going to start booking in the next few weeks.

And I have to be honest, our fundraising has taken a hit as we've switched to an all-digital campaign. Now we're just 45 days out from our election, and we're staring down our first fundraising quarter filing.

This is the only major fundraising deadline before our special election, so a lot of attention will be paid to our numbers. Especially since everyone saw the _Cook Political Report_ downgrade our race to just Lean Democrat from Likely Democrat.

**We need the resources to fight back and end this quarter strong.**

**[If you are able, can you make a donation right now toward our big $25,000 end-of-quarter goal?](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8f0/29776869/a1c2e58/569fa74c/3485736739/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMjdfRU9RMS1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_eoq?refcode=em20_200327_EOQ1-full")** [DONATE](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8f0/29776869/a1c2e58/569fa74c/3485736739/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMjdfRU9RMS1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_eoq?refcode=em20_200327_EOQ1-full") We know it's not an opportune time to ask for support -- but we need to focus on the big picture of this situation. **We NEED to have good leaders in office who will do the right thing when we're in a crisis.**

I know, you know, and so many people know that the only candidate in this race who will do that is Christy -- but we still have to get Christy's name and message out to a lot of voters in the district.

**[We have a goal of raising $25,000 from online fundraising by the end of this quarter -- only 4 days away. If you can, chip in $5 or whatever you can afford to our campaign.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8f0/29776869/a1c2e58/569fa74c/3485736739/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMjdfRU9RMS1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_eoq?refcode=em20_200327_EOQ1-full")** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | [Express Donate $5](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8f0/29776869/a1c2e58/569fa74d/3485736739/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMjdfRU9RMS1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_eoq?refcode=em20_200327_EOQ1-full&express_lane=true&amount=5") | [Express Donate $25](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8f0/29776869/a1c2e58/569fa74e/3485736739/VEsD/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMjdfRU9RMS1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_eoq?refcode=em20_200327_EOQ1-full&express_lane=true&amount=25")

[Express Donate $250](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8f0/29776869/a1c2e58/569fa749/3485736739/VEsO/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMjdfRU9RMS1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_eoq?refcode=em20_200327_EOQ1-full&express_lane=true&amount=250") | [Donate Other Amount](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b8f0/29776869/a1c2e58/569fa74c/3485736739/VEsP/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMjdfRU9RMS1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2003_email_christysmith_eoq?refcode=em20_200327_EOQ1-full")
Thank you so much, Peter. When we say we can't do this without you, **we mean it.**

\-- Lexie

Lexie Kelly
Finance Director

Hi friend,
Today, the House of Representatives passed a $2.2 trillion coronavirus stimulus package that is now headed to the President’s desk. The package is an important step to provide some much-needed relief to our hospitals, our communities, and our economy — we applaud this bi-partisan effort during the crisis. **However, we must continue to demand that any financial assistance to industries is contingent upon companies cleaning up their environmental act. Join over 21,973 people who signed our petition for a clean recovery package:**

Friend, As a business owner for more than 30 years, **the biggest lesson I learned is that with the evolving world we live in, one must always be ready to adapt to their environment.** Our campaign has always been a grassroots movement as we have reached thousands of voters across Oklahoma's 5th district through our events and field efforts. While we have benefited from this encouraging momentum, it's no longer enough. As more and more people are encouraged to remain at home, **it 's important for our campaign to shift its focus to bring our message directly to the voters.** We need to step up our presence online and cut through the negative agenda of the liberal, mainstream media. But to increase our digital footprint, we need more resources. # 56 times.
**The COVID-19 pandemic has ground the American economy to a halt and jeopardized the life and health of every American. **
**While Americans focus on the safety of their families and communities, the public records of their elected leaders continue to be defined by choices they are making right now, as well as over the past few years when warnings were being sounded by public health experts.**

**Here is the sad truth about my opponent, Adam Kinzinger: He voted not once, not twice, but _56 times to eliminate the Prevention and Public Health Fund that helps the CDC fight pandemics like COVID-19. _**

**The funds he voted 56 times to eliminate are the very funds on which our lives and our livelihoods now depend.

IL-16 cannot afford Adam Kinzinger any longer. [Can I count on you to chip in today to help us turn this seat from red to blue?](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=e7a0f4b8fc&e=140a9b81e7)**

Dani Brzozowski
Democratic Nominee for Congress, IL 16

clean_body: 56 times. That's how many times my opponent voted to eliminate fund that the CDC uses to help fight pandemics like COVID-19 | | | | | | | 56 times. The COVID-19 pandemic has ground the American economy to a halt and jeopardized the life and health of every American. While Americans focus on the safety of their families and communities, the public records of their elected leaders continue to be defined by choices they are making right now, as well as over the past few years when warnings were being sounded by public health experts. Here is the sad truth about my opponent, Adam Kinzinger: He voted not once, not twice, but 56 times to eliminate the Prevention and Public Health Fund that helps the CDC fight pandemics like COVID-19. The funds he voted 56 times to eliminate are the very funds on which our lives and our livelihoods now depend. IL-16 cannot afford Adam Kinzinger any longer. Dani Brzozowski Democratic Nominee for Congress, IL 16 | "Donate Now" | | | | | | | | | | | daniforillinois.com | | Our mailing address is: Dani for Illinois PO Box 1671 LaSalle, IL 61301 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or .

subject: World War C
body:
Talking with Obama CDC Director Dr. Tom Frieden

| Friend, Right now, the voices of our public health experts are more important than ever. That's why we wanted to share "World War C: Us Against the Microbe," a podcast episode published today featuring Dr. Tom Frieden, CEO and President of Resolve to Save Lives, and former head of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention under President Obama. subject: The Heart of Hoosiers
body:
Yesterday at his daily press conference, Governor Holcomb said something that stuck with me. He said, "It's simply amazing to see the heart of...

View this email in your browser (https://us11.campaign-archive.com/?e=1acd9bb3d6&u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8d0da69c6d)

The Heart of Hoosiers

Yesterday at his daily press conference, Governor Holcomb said something that stuck with me. He said, "It's simply amazing to see the heart of Hoosiers who are hunkered down right now, not slowing down to meet the needs of their neighbors."

Hunkered down...but not slowing down. Across the state, we're seeing stories of Hoosiers stepping up with incredible acts of service to our neighbors. It's stories of companies shifting production -- distilleries making hand sanitizer, manufacturers making personal protective equipment, stories of generous tips on take-out, and stories of people making calls to look in on their fellow Hoosiers.

If you need to lift up, check out Govenror Holcomb's campaign Twitter page (https://twitter.com/holcombforin?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) for some really fantastic stories.

That's because we know that while we're social distancing, we're really all in this together. And I'm thankful every day that we're being led through it all by the decisive leadership of Governor Holcomb.

With Governor Holcomb's leadership and the heart of Hoosiers, I know we'll get through this stronger than ever.

- Chairman Kyle Hupfer
Governor Holcomb's Week That Was

Keeping Hoosiers Safe: Coronavirus

Throughout the week, Governor Holcomb provided daily updates to Hoosiers about how Indiana is responding to the coronavirus pandemic.

Check out some of the actions Governor Holcomb and his team have taken below to keep Hoosiers safe:
* In order to keep vulnerable Hoosiers safe, Governor Holcomb has asked us to hunker down and stay at home. Traffic on Indiana roads was down 41% yesterday compared to the same day last year.
* Access to Indiana's great government service continues remotely, including welfare and unemployment applications.
* Governor Holcomb is continuing to enforce carry-out and delivery options only for restaurants in Indiana. Please continue to support your local restaurants options during this time.
* Restaurants with liquor licenses can sell alcohol for carryout consumption.
* The Department of Workforce Development has suspended the one-week waiting period that is required before paying unemployment benefits.
* Department of Correction inmates are now working on producing personal protective equipment, including face masks and protective gowns.
* The Indiana State Department of Education (DOE) and Family and Social Services Administration (FSSA) are teaming up to encourage schools to provide limited child care services to the children of emergency and other essential workers.
* Hoosiers with chronic health issues will be able to receive a 90-day supply of their non-controlled prescription medication.
* The Indiana Economic Development Corporation has spoken with more than 1,000 businesses and economic development agencies in the last five days about ways to continue supporting the state’s economy. More than 135 companies have been fully vetted as being able to help, such as shifting production to make necessary medical and protective equipment.
* The Indiana National Guard are being deployed to six food banks across the state to help sort and pack food for distribution.
* The Office of Community and Rural Affairs announced Community Development Block Grant funds may be redirected to assist with COVID-19 needs, such as assistance for small businesses.

To stay up to date on the latest developments, please make you're following Governor Holcomb on Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/HolcombForIndiana/?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , Twitter (https://twitter.com/holcombforin?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) and Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/holcombforin/?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) !

As we all continue to fight the coronavirus in Indiana, please remember to wash your hands frequently and to continue to practice social distancing. In order to flatten the curve and protect vulnerable Hoosiers, it's going to take a team effort. We're all in this together!
Resources: A State That Works Together

As news and information on the coronavirus continue to rapidly change -- we want to make sure you have the resources you need to stay up to date!
* Indiana State Department of Health - The Indiana State Department of Health is posting important information, and frequent updates to their website -- as well as offering an Indiana coronavirus map, and other resources for Hoosiers! Check out their website here (https://coronavirus.in.gov?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) !
* Indiana Coronavirus Hotline - Get help with any questions you have regarding COVID-19 via Indiana's toll-free, 24/7 hotline! Call 877-826-0011!
* FAQ's on Indiana's Stay-at-Home Order -- This is definitely a new challenge for all of us. Click here to help answer some of your questions (https://calendar.in.gov/site/gov/event/indiana-stay-at-home-order-faq/?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) !
* File for Unemployment - Many Hoosiers are temporarily unemployed due to COVID-19. Click here for information and to file for unemployment (https://www.in.gov/dwd/3474.htm?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) !

We're going to get through this together! Help one another, and share these resources with friends and family who need them!
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScXgFP5TNAn30MOcbD6sPcQ0-WW0U_zJhGd8sbOKCj0mMCaBQ/viewform?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Next Week: Indiana GOP Virtual Phone Bank!

As we're all hunkered down at home and practicing social distancing, we have a great way you can connect with fellow Hoosiers and support Governor Holcomb and your fellow Republican team at the same time.

We'll be hosting an Indiana GOP Virtual Phone Bank on Wednesday, April 1 from 5 p.m. - 8 p.m!

Sign up here (https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScXgFP5TNAn30MOcbD6sPcQ0-WW0U_zJhGd8sbOKCj0mMCaBQ/viewform?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , and a member of our team will reach out to help get you connected with the technology you'll need to help reach your fellow Hoosiers from home.

--> Click here to sign up for our virtual phone bank <-- (https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScXgFP5TNAn30MOcbD6sPcQ0-WW0U_zJhGd8sbOKCj0mMCaBQ/viewform?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

Congress Passes Relief for Americans

This week, Congress passed the CARES Act, a $2 trillion package to help Americans, and it was just signed by President Donald Trump!

The CARES Act includes:
* $500 billion in emergency loans for businesses & municipalities that have been affected by the coronavirus.
* $350 million for small business to help them meet payroll and keep Americans employed.
* Will increase unemployment by $600 per week for 4 months.
* Americans will receive a one-time check of $1,200 and $500 per child.
* $150 billion is dedicated to hospitals and medical centers treating coronavirus patients.

Check out what Senators Todd Young and Mike Braun had to say about the CARES Act below:
* Senator Young: “This emergency relief package is designed to help Hoosier families, workers, job creators, healthcare heroes on the front lines, patients, and everyone who has suffered through no fault of their own. Now that an agreement has been reached, we must immediately get this critical relief into the hands of people who need it most...We are going to get through this by working together, and we are going to come out stronger.”
* Senator Braun: "The coronavirus impacts our health and our economy, and I will be supporting this assistance package which provides massive assistance for our hospitals, reaches out to directly help workers keep their jobs & give immediate help to those who lost their jobs, and provides the largest Main Street assistance program in history for small business owners to keep their doors open, which I was personally involved in securing.”

News You Can Use

Governor Eric Holcomb sets example of leadership (https://www.cnhinews.com/indiana/opinion/article_778a059b-c297-5ba7-9e68-eb54169d7346.html?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID])
Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch highlights efforts to help rural communities with COVID-19 (https://wkvi.com/2020/03/lt-governor-highlights-efforts-to-help-farmers-rural-communities-deal-with-covid-19/?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID])
Senator Todd Young says small business loans may be available soon (https://www.kpcnews.com/covid-19/article_fefb6d67-2a17-5922-a4a7-f960f6b87818.html?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID])
Senator Mike Braun says coronavirus limits are best set at the local level (https://www.journalgazette.net/news/local/indiana/20200325/braun-virus-limits-best-set-at-local-level?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID])
Congresswoman Jackie Walorski expects president to sign relief bill (https://www.wibc.com/news/local-indiana/walorski-says-relief-package-will-pass-house-expects-president-to-sign-it/?mc_cid=8d0da69c6d&mc_eid=[UNIQID])
